What you'll do Originate new AI and ML services opportunities through targeted outbound, warm introductions, partner referrals, and inbound lead follow-up Build and manage a qualified pipeline across enterprise and mid-market accounts in the US and Canada Run technical discovery in partnership with our solutions and engineering leadership, converting business problems into defensible engagement scopes Shape proposals, SOWs, and commercial terms that reflect the real complexity of AI implementation work Navigate multi-stakeholder buying processes involving technical evaluators, finance, legal, and executive sponsors Maintain accurate forecasting and pipeline hygiene in HubSpot Represent Azumo 's engineering credibility in client conversations, not just our company story Requirements What we're looking for Solid experience selling technology services or custom software engineering engagements to US-based buyers. A track record of originating new logo business, not only expanding existing accounts Working fluency in AI and ML sufficient to hold a substantive first call with a technical buyer. You do not need to be an engineer, but you do need to speak credibly about model selection, LLM applications, data engineering, and production deployment concerns Experience carrying quota against six-figure and low-seven-figure deal sizes with multi-stakeholder decision processes Strong discovery skills. You lead with questions, not pitches Disciplined about CRM hygiene, forecasting, and pipeline reviews Written and verbal communication that holds up in front of senior technical and executive audiences Based in the United States and authorized to work in the US Nice to have Prior experience selling nearshore or distributed engineering services Existing relationships with CTOs, VPs of Engineering, or Heads of Data at mid-market or enterprise companies Background in consulting, engineering, or another technical discipline before moving into sales Benefits Company benefits include: Paid Time Off Mentored Career Development U.S. Holidays USD Remuneration Profit Sharing Maternity Coverage 100% Remote Compensation and location Remote within the United States. Compensation is commensurate with experience and includes base salary plus commission. About Azumo Azumo is a software engineering partner for the era of AI. We have shipped production AI work for Meta, Discovery Channel, and dozens of other companies building real products on AI foundations. We are a services company, which means our credibility is measured by what we deliver, not by what we pitch. This role exists because we are seeing more demand for AI implementation work than we currently have capacity to originate through our inbound only go-to-market strategy. jobs.title: NOPP Project Management Support
jobs.description: Overview Lynker Corporation is seeking to hire a qualified candidate to support a contract with NOAA Research Office of Ocean Exploration and Research (OER). These are full-time positions that may be performed on-site or remotely, with anticipated travel in support of meetings, workshops, and field activities. Hiring for these positions will be contingent on contract award. Lynker will be operating under its Propel joint venture, a NOAA ProTech Oceans 2.0 prime contract holder. This Contract is Contingent on Contract Award. The contractor shall provide project management and inter-agency coordination services to facilitate the National Oceanographic Partnership Program (NOPP) and its partnership with the U.S. Navy Office of Naval Research (ONR). Responsibilities Duties of the NOPP Project Management Support will include the following: Inter-agency Coordination: Planning and coordinating between NOPP and ONR representatives, including managing Inter-agency Agreement (IAA) drafts and facilitating and planning monthly Inter-agency Work Group (IWG) meetings, to include the preparation of meeting agendas and minutes, to ensure cross-agency strategic alignment. Financial and Spend Planning: Coordinating with the OER Administration and Finance Division to document project funding transfers and formulating the annual NOPP spend plan to ensure fiscal alignment. Reporting and Outreach: Coordinating the development of the NOPP Annual Report and executing outreach initiatives to expand program visibility; and briefing leadership on inter-agency coordination status during monthly internal OER-NOPP meetings. Programmatic Services: Coordinating with the NOAA science programs and laboratories for options and funding opportunities Qualifications About Lynker Lynker is a growing, employee owned business, specializing in professional, scientific and technical services. Our continually expanding team combines scientific expertise with mature, results-driven processes and tools to achieve technically sound, cost effective solutions in hydrology/water sciences, geospatial analysis, information technology, resource management, conservation, and management and business process improvement. We focus on putting the right people in the right place to be effective. And having the right people is critical for success. Our streamlined organization enables and empowers our talented professionals to tackle our customers' scientific and technical priorities – creatively and effectively. Lynker offers a team-oriented work environment, and the opportunity to work in a culture of exceptionally skilled professionals who embrace sound science and creative solutions. Lynker's benefits include the following: Comprehensive healthcare for the employee at no monthly cost Healthcare benefit covers medical, prescription drug, dental, and vision Personal Time Off (PTO) Policy plus paid holidays Highly competitive compensation plan regularly calibrated against industry and location benchmarks 401(k) retirement plan with company-matching Employee Stock Ownership Plan (ESOP) – we're all company owners! Flexible spending accounts Employee assistance program (EAP) Short- and long-term disability insurance Life and accident insurance Tuition assistance/Training/Workforce improvement reimbursement per year Spot bonuses for exceptional performance Annual Employee Recognition Awards with bonuses Employee Referral Program Free centralized, self-directed Learning Management System to learn at your own pace Personalized career growth plans for every employee Lynker is an E-Verify employer. Lynker is an equal opportunity employer and makes all employment decisions based on merit, qualifications, and business needs. jobs.title: Développeur logiciel sénior (C# .Net, AWS) H/F
jobs.description: Qui sommes-nous ? N2JSoft est une fintech B2B SaaS spécialisée dans la digitalisation des processus RH et financiers. Avec plus de 20 000 clients et 1 million d’utilisateurs dans 98 pays, nos solutions N2F et Keeple s’imposent comme des références sur le marché français. N2F est une solution complète de gestion des dépenses professionnelles (notes de frais, cartes corporate, factures fournisseurs), et Keeple centralise la gestion RH du quotidien (congés, absences, temps de travail) le tout dans une interface simple, intuitive et 100% connectée (app web et mobile). Portés par une équipe engagée, qui place l’humain au centre de ses priorités, nous poursuivons une ambition claire : devenir le leader européen de la gestion des dépenses professionnelles. Être Développeur sénior H/F chez N2JSOFT, c’est : Rejoindre une équipe transverse à fort impact, responsable de la modernisation de la stack et de la diffusion des bonnes pratiques techniques dans l’organisation. Tu seras au centre des grandes décisions d’architecture, de la refonte du legacy vers une architecture modulaire, tout en gardant un œil sur les problématiques de delivery et de qualité. Tes principales missions : Contribuer à l’évolution de la stack technique avec un fort focus sur la scalabilité, la cloudification et l’ excellence opérationnelle Accompagner les squads dans la refonte modulaire et la modernisation du code legacy Prototyper et partager les bonnes pratiques : clean architecture , tests, sécurité, observabilité, etc. Participer aux décisions d’ architecture logicielle , challenger les choix techniques en lien avec les enjeux produit et delivery Diagnostiquer et résoudre des problématiques techniques complexes Stack & environnement technique : Langage : C# (Latest), .NET 10 Architecture : Monolithe (legacy) & Monolithe modulaire Cloud : AWS (EKS, SQS/SNS, DynamoDB…) CI/CD : Github Actions, Terraform (IaC), ArgoCD Qualité : Tests automatisés, analyse statique (Qodana), code review rigoureuse Observabilité : OpenTelemetry, Grafana, Loki Front : Angular (latest), HTML5/CSS3, TypeScript, SPA Requirements Ce qu’on attend de toi : Maîtrise avancée de C# / .NET Core Solides compétences en développement web (Angular, SPA , HTML/CSS, TypeScript) Bonne compréhension de l’ architecture modulaire , DDD, CQRS, Clean Architecture et MicroServices Expérience concrète en environnement cloud , idéalement AWS À l’aise avec Docker , GitHub, et les CI/CD modernes Sensibilité forte à la qualité du code , sécurité , performance et observabilité Capacité à mettre en place des tests automatisés (Test unitaires, intégrations, …) Les petits plus qui font la différence : Culture DevOps / SRE Connaissance d’AngularJS (pour les migrations) Expérience dans la migration de code legacy À l’aise sur Linux en environnement de prod La pratique de services comme SQS/SNS, EKS ou DynamoDB Des connaissances sur Kubernetes Pourquoi nous rejoindre ? Une équipe Core qui a un impact global sur la tech et l’organisation Des défis techniques stimulants dans une culture de la qualité et du partage Un cadre de travail flexible : télétravail complet possible avec une semaine de présence par trimestre Une vraie autonomie dans les choix techniques, et du temps pour faire bien Les avantages chez N2JSOFT : Prime de vacances, Chèques cadeaux à Noël, Chèques rentrée scolaire (si tu as des enfants), Chèque culture, Cours de musique, Événements d’équipe toute l’année. #N2JSOFTFR Originally posted on Himalayas

jobs.title: Service Operations Representative
jobs.description: About RoadRunner At RoadRunner , our daily motivation is to accelerate the road to Zero Waste™ while delivering maximum value for our customers and communities through efficient, scalable, and sustainable waste operations. Our team cares deeply about each other and our planet, and we are dedicated to leveraging technology and innovation to modernize a vital industry with integrity, expertise, and a passion for creating a future where waste is a problem of the past™. Knowledge: Maintain understanding of all relevant process changes, service updates, and new guidelines 15% - Vendor Relations & Service Coordination External Communication: Serve as the main point of contact for external hauling partners (vendors), communicating customer needs, coordinating scheduling updates, and securing successful service adherence. Internal Collaboration: Collaborate effectively with internal Customer Success, Sales, and Operations teams to understand service requ

jobs.title: Fleet Key Account Manager Europe (temporary)
jobs.description: About Talixo Talixo is the world's leading operator of pre-booked transfers, working with the biggest brands in travel. We partner with fleets in 1,100+ cities and 130+ countries worldwide to provide travellers with reliable, high-quality rides, making their journeys easier and more enjoyable, from door to door. Since 2012, we've been helping travel brands create more connected journeys, and we're looking for people willing to go the extra mile. Operating across two key locations: Krakow and Berlin, our cohesive teams - IT, Operations, Fleet, Pricing, Sales, Finance and HR - drive technological innovation, operational excellence, and organizational development. With a shared focus on refining processes and enhancing technology, while spearheading strategic growth initiatives, we seamlessly contribute to our collective success. We are seeking passionate professionals to join our team in Europe, as a Fleet Key Account Manager, to manage business relations with our Taxi and Limousine Fleet Partners network within several cluster countries in European region. The role is fully remote. Responsibilities: Relationship Management: Develop and maintain strong, long-lasting relationships with key fleet partners. Act as the main point of contact for all account-related matters, ensuring effective communication and understanding of fleet requirements. Strategic Account Planning: Collaborate with internal teams to create and implement strategic account plans tailored to each fleet customer's needs and integrate them in Talixo systems. Identify opportunities for account growth and expansion, ensuring alignment with the company's overall business objectives. Contract Negotiation and Management: Shortlist potential new partner fleets, make cold calls to acquire new leads, and qualify leads. Establishing a network of partnerships and negotiating deals across your region and your product line. Onboarding and training of new fleets. Data Analysis and Reporting: Utilise data and analytics to track key performance indicators, providing insights and recommendations to enhance fleet services and quality. Prepare regular reports for fleets, highlighting performance metrics and identifying areas for improvement Market Intelligence: Stay informed about industry trends, competitor activities, and market changes to identify opportunities and potential risks. Close collaboration with other Departments (Sales, Tech, Operations) to improve processes, and procedures and further develop our IT system and tools. This role will be reporting to Regional Head of Fleet, located within European time zone. Requirements: Bachelor's degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field. Minimum of 3 years of experience in the procurement field or sales. Strong negotiation, communication, and interpersonal skills with intercultural experience. Proficiency in data analysis and trend identification to drive informed business decisions. Demonstrated ability to approach problem-solving in a structured manner, showcasing independence and creativity. Excellent organizational and time-management skills. Familiarity with CRM software and other relevant tools. Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in English - C1 is a must, Greek is nice to have. What we offer: 100% remote working, Home Office, flexible working arrangements. Fixed-term cooperation, or possible B2B cooperation. Salary structure: monthly salary base + commission. Business Travel expenses reimbursement. Discounts for Talixo services. Additional 3 paid days of per year, on the top of the standard allowance. Recruitment process: CV screening and candidate selection Screening call on Google Meet with Recruiter (only selected candidates) 1st stage Google Meet interview with Hiring Manager (only selected candidates) 2nd stage Google Meet interview with Hiring Manager and COO + Case Study (only selected candidates) Feedback We kindly ask all candidates to submit their applications in English. jobs.title: Telehealth Doctor
jobs.description: * Help patients along their health journey via telehealth * Fully remote/work from home role with flexible working hours * Work with a friendly and talented team G’day, we’re Mosh Our mission is to get people the best possible health outcomes through best-in class health programs. We know the current healthcare system is not meeting the needs of the modern people, and we reckon there’s a better, more holistic way. Our solutions for hair loss, sexual health, skin care, mental health and weight management are easy, affordable and completely discreet. More than a job There’s nothing better than the feeling that what you’re doing matters and makes a difference. We’re pretty lucky this is the norm and not the exception for us. Responsibilities include: Calling/video conferencing with new and existing patients to provide education, advice and assistance Managing existing patient’s treatment plans, including assessing their response to current treatment and prescribing appropriate medications and making changes as appropriate and within scope of practice Triaging presenting symptoms to the relevant medical practitioner/pharmacist Liaising with managers, medical practitioners, pharmacists, allied health professionals and other employees Sharing feedback for constant improvement of systems and processes to improve patient experience What you’ll bring AHPRA General Registration as a PGY 5 + doctor 2 years of clinical work experience in Australia Must be based in Australia What’s on offer We hire teammates that share our values, believe in our mission and are passionate about the future of Aussies' health. jobs.title: Digital Marketing Specialist
jobs.description: Overview High Tide is a leading cannabis retailer and eCommerce company with a portfolio of brands across North America and Europe. Our portfolio includes Canna Cabana , one of Canada's largest cannabis retail chains, along with leading eCommerce brands including Smoke Cartel, Daily High Club, FAB CBD, NuLeaf Naturals, and more. We're looking for a Digital Marketing Specialist who loves bringing marketing initiatives to life and thrives in a fast-paced environment where no two days are the same. You'll work across multiple brands and digital channels, supporting email marketing, affiliate growth, paid media, website marketing initiatives, customer insights, and promotional campaigns that reach millions of customers. Two key areas of focus for this role are email marketing and affiliate marketing. You'll own email campaign calendar management across multiple brands, ensuring timelines stay on track and stakeholders deliver required inputs on schedule. You'll also manage affiliate programs, including partner recruitment, relationship management, campaign coordination, and performance reporting. In addition, you'll support paid media initiatives, customer surveys, website marketing campaigns, and broader digital marketing projects while working closely with the Digital Marketing Manager to help drive growth across the business. High Tide does not operate a physical office in Brazil. English fluency is required. Resumes must be submitted in English. Portfolios are ideally submitted in English; however, Portuguese portfolios will also be accepted. Responsibilities Email Marketing Own and maintain email marketing calendars across multiple brands. Serve as the primary coordinator for campaign timelines, ensuring dependencies across teams are completed on schedule. Coordinate campaign timelines with Category Management, Pricing, Creative, eCommerce, Analytics, and Marketing teams to ensure campaigns are ready for launch. Support the planning, QA, scheduling, and deployment of email campaigns. Monitor campaign performance and report on key email marketing KPIs. Support promotional, retention, lifecycle, and automated email marketing initiatives. Analyze campaign results and recommend opportunities to improve engagement, conversion, and revenue performance. Support A/B testing initiatives and ongoing email optimization efforts. Maintain campaign documentation and workflows. Affiliate Marketing Manage and grow affiliate marketing programs across multiple brands. Serve as a primary day-to-day contact for affiliate partners, publishers, and affiliate networks. Maintain strong relationships with affiliate partners and identify opportunities to increase performance. Proactively identify, recruit, and onboard new affiliate partners, publishers, influencers, and strategic partnerships Coordinate promotional opportunities and campaign execution with affiliate partners. Ensure affiliates receive creative assets, campaign details, and promotional calendars on time. Monitor affiliate partner performance, revenue contribution, and overall ROI. Analyze affiliate performance data and identify opportunities for growth. Support affiliate program growth through outreach, relationship building, and promotional opportunities. Prepare weekly and monthly affiliate reporting. Paid Media & Performance Marketing Support the planning, execution, and optimization of paid media campaigns across digital channels. Monitor campaign performance and provide recommendations to improve ROAS, CPA, CTR, conversion rates, and revenue. Coordinate campaign requirements, audience targeting needs, and creative assets with internal teams and external partners. Develop creative briefs and campaign requirements for designers, copywriters, and external partners. Analyze campaign results and identify opportunities to improve perf

jobs.title: Independent End Point Assessor- Facilities Services Operative
jobs.description: Overview Flexible Worker Contract Location: Remote (West Midlands or Southwest or Southeast ) About the role We are looking for an Independent End Point Assessor (IEPA) with a background in Facilities Management or Facilities Services , who is passionate about developing others to deliver high-quality End Point Assessment (EPA) for the Level 2 Facilities Services Operative apprenticeship standard. This is a fantastic opportunity for a facilities manager, or industry professional to use their expertise to support apprentices to successfully complete their qualifications. As an End Point Assessor, you’ll play a critical role in ensuring apprentices meet occupational standards, applying your professional judgement to assess competence and performance. How you'll make an impact: Delivering End Point Assessments (EPA) in line with the Level 2 Facilities Services Operative standard Assess apprentices using a range of methods including observation, professional discussion, and review of evidence Provide clear, accurate and constructive feedback and grading decisions Use EPA platforms and systems to manage and record assessment activity Participate in standardisation and quality assurance activities Engage with training providers, employers and apprentices We’d love to hear from you if you offer the following: A recognised Assessor qualification (A1, CAVA, or equivalent) or working towards Experience in Facilities management, Facilities Services, or related environments Knowledge of areas such as maintenance, front of house / reception services, portering and logistics, post room operations Experience of producing clear and accurate reports The flexibility to travel for face-to-face assessments when required Excellent IT skills using platform such as Teams, Outlook and SharePoint Feeling inspired? Read the full Job Description to find out more. Why work as a flexible worker for NCFE ? Our flexible workforce provides supportive quality assurance to ensure that learners taking NCFE qualifications are getting the best education possible. If you become a flexible worker with us, you’ll choose how to fit your work around your other personal and professional commitments – making this a great opportunity to make a difference in a way that suits you. You’ll receive full training and the materials, tools or equipment needed to ensure you’re able to complete your duties in line with NCFE requirements, with the ability to take on flexible worker contract alongside other work, without the need to be registered as self-employed. All applicants will be expected to adhere to NCFE ’s behavioural framework and to uphold our core values. You can find out more about how our flexible arrangements work, the flexible worker roles we recruit, and frequently asked questions around the application, induction and training process, and your rights and protections by visiting our Flexible worker opportunities page. Shape real change with an NCFE career Imagine a career where your contributions affect not only what people learn, but the way that learning is developed and delivered. With over 170 years of education experience our core purpose remains at the heart of the organisation – to promote and advance learning to create a fairer, more inclusive society, making sure no learner is left behind. Want to know more? Take a look at our website https://www.ncfe.org.uk/ How to apply Before applying, please note that we require all candidates to be able to demonstrate proof of Right to Work in the UK. Ready to join us and shape real change? Apply with your CV and answer the associated application questions. Closing Date: Friday 24 July 2026 Interview Date: TBC Please note that at interview stage you’ll be required to share any certificates with us as outlined in the essential criteria for the role If successful in this post you will be required to complete an Enhanced Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) check. jobs.title: Public Relations & Account Coordinator

jobs.title: Business Development Manager - Mobility Solutions
jobs.description: Trafi (an Enghouse Company) is a leading provider of journey planning solutions, enabling cities and transit agencies to optimize urban transportation. Our innovative platform integrates various transport modes, offering real-time data and user-friendly applications that enhance the commuter experience and improve city mobility. We are committed to shaping the future of mobility in urban environments through intelligent and efficient solutions. We are looking for a commercially driven, entrepreneurial sales professional who thrives on building opportunities where others see uncertainty. This isn't a role for someone who simply responds to published tenders or manages an existing pipeline. We're looking for someone who understands where the mobility market is heading, builds trusted relationships with cities, transit agencies and industry partners, and helps position Trafi as the partner that gets them there. Working closely with Product, Delivery and Leadership teams, you'll help shape long-term commercial opportunities, influence our product roadmap through customer insights, and drive the adoption of solutions that improve urban mobility. This is a Remote role based in Germany, offering the flexibility to work from home or the office (in Leipzig) according to your preference. Responsibilities: Develop and execute commercial strategies to identify and grow new business opportunities. Build long-term relationships with cities, transit agencies, operators, consultants, and strategic partners. Identify emerging market trends, customer challenges, and competitive developments, turning them into commercial opportunities. Lead complex consultative sales processes, including public tenders where applicable. Collaborate closely with Product, Delivery, and Marketing teams, ensuring customer insights help shape future product development. Develop compelling proposals, business cases, and presentations that clearly communicate the value of Trafi's solutions. Represent Trafi at industry events, conferences, and networking opportunities. Maintain an accurate sales pipeline, forecasts, and account plans. Qualifications and Experience: 3+ years of experience in business development, enterprise sales, account management, or partnerships within mobility, transportation, SaaS, or another complex B2B technology environment. Experience building relationships with public sector organizations, transit agencies, operators, or regulated industries. Strong consultative selling skills and experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder sales cycles. An entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to identify opportunities before they become formal procurement projects. Strong understanding of urban mobility trends and a genuine interest in shaping the future of transportation. Experience with public tenders is an advantage. Excellent communication, negotiation, and presentation skills. Ability to work independently while collaborating across cross-functional teams. jobs.title: Actuary - Primary Care Organization
jobs.description: Become a part of our caring community The Actuary, Analytics/Forecasting analyzes and forecasts financial, economic, and other data to provide accurate and timely information for strategic and operational decisions. Establishes metrics, provides data analyses, and works directly to support business intelligence. Evaluates industry, economic, financial, and market trends to forecast the organization's short, medium and long-term financial and competitive position. The Actuary, Analytics/Forecasting works on problems of diverse scope and complexity ranging from moderate to substantial. The Actuary, Primary Care Organization (PCO) is responsible for supporting actuarial analytics, forecasting, data integrity, and business intelligence initiatives. This role develops and executes processes and controls to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of data used in actuarial analysis and decision-making. The Actuary collaborates with business partners and stakeholders to understand business needs, identify issues, troubleshoot data anomalies, conduct root cause analyses, and develop cost-effective solutions. This position advises leaders and executives on matters of significance, including segment-specific strategies, financial trends, medical claims drivers, and the organization’s short-, medium-, and long-term financial and competitive position. This role works under minimal supervision, exercises independent judgment, and makes decisions on complex actuarial matters requiring analysis of variable factors and determination of the appropriate course of action. *Sponsorship is not available for this role Use your skills to make an impact Core Responsibilities Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business needs, clarify issues, and translate actuarial findings into actionable business insights. Analyze medical claims trends and identify key drivers impacting financial performance, utilization, cost, and business outcomes. Develop, maintain, and execute processes and controls to ensure data integrity, accuracy, and consistency across actuarial analyses and reporting. Troubleshoot data anomalies, perform root cause analysis, and recommend cost-effective resolutions to improve data quality and business intelligence. Analyze and forecast financial, economic, and business data to support strategic and operational decision-making. Apply actuarial modeling, technical expertise, and industry knowledge to evaluate the organization’s financial and competitive position over the short, medium, and long term. Provide strategic guidance to executives and business leaders on matters of significance, including segment-specific actuarial and financial strategies. Prepare, present, and document actuarial analyses, assumptions, findings, and recommendations for leadership and key stakeholders. Exercise independent judgment on complex actuarial issues, evaluating variable factors and determining appropriate courses of action with minimal supervision. Establish metrics, monitor trends, and deliver data-driven insights that support business planning, forecasting, reporting, pricing, reserving, rate filing, and trend analysis initiatives. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree from an accredited University in the USA Member of the American Academy of Actuaries, also known as MAAA Fellow of the Society of Actuaries, also known as FSA ; or Associate of the Society of Actuaries, also known as ASA , plus one or more of the following: Experience working as an Actuary in one or more of the following insurance areas: oMedicaid oMedicare Advantage oCommercial insurance Experience in more than three actuarial functions, such as: oModeling oPricing oRate filing oReporting and analysis oReserving oTrending Demonstrated ability to conduct actuarial analysis, interpret complex data, and provide recommendations that support business decision-making. Strong ability to present findings, recommendations, and actuarial analyses to stakeholders and leadershi

jobs.title: IoT Platform Engineer
jobs.description: Bright Vision Technologies is a technology consulting and software development company delivering cloud, AI, data, and enterprise solutions across the United States. This is a fantastic opportunity to join an established and well-respected organization offering tremendous career growth potential. Job Title: IoT Platform Engineer Location: 100% Remote (U.S.) Position Type: Full-time, Direct W2 Salary Range: $100,000–$150,000 Annually Experience Required: 6+ years Sponsorship: U.S. Citizens, Green Card Holders, EAD Holders, and H-1B transfer candidates are encouraged to apply. We are unable to sponsor new H-1B visa petitions for this position. Job Summary: We are seeking an experienced IoT Platform Engineer to design and build connected, data-driven industrial systems spanning the factory floor to enterprise cloud platforms. In this role you will architect end-to-end IIoT solutions, including edge data acquisition, secure connectivity, cloud ingestion, real-time analytics, and operational dashboards. The ideal candidate will combine strong software engineering skills with practical experience in industrial protocols, edge computing, and cloud IoT platforms. In this role you will work closely with cross-functional partners — product, design, engineering, operations, and business stakeholders — to translate ambiguous requirements into well-engineered solutions, and will be expected to raise the bar through code review, design review, and mentorship of more junior engineers. The successful candidate brings strong engineering discipline, a clear communication style, and a track record of shipping meaningful work that holds up well in production. Key Responsibilities Architect end-to-end IIoT solutions spanning edge devices, gateways, connectivity, and cloud platforms. Design data acquisition pipelines from PLCs, SCADA, and sensors using protocols such as OPC UA, Modbus, MQTT, and Sparkplug B. Build edge computing applications using AWS IoT Greengrass, Azure IoT Edge, or similar platforms. Implement secure connectivity using mTLS, certificate management, and zero-trust patterns. Build cloud ingestion and processing pipelines using AWS IoT Core, Azure IoT Hub, Kafka, or similar. Develop real-time analytics, anomaly detection, and predictive maintenance models that turn raw telemetry from industrial assets into actionable signals for operators and reliability engineers. Build operational dashboards and alerts for plant managers and operators. Integrate IIoT data streams with MES, ERP, CMMS, and historian systems, designing reliable bidirectional flows that maintain consistency between operational technology and enterprise systems of record. Implement strong device management, including provisioning, OTA updates, and certificate rotation. Collaborate closely with OT, process engineering, and reliability teams, gathering requirements, aligning on technical approach, and ensuring that delivered solutions meet both functional needs and broader engineering and operational standards. Ensure compliance with relevant industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443, NIST). Maintain comprehensive, current technical documentation — including architecture diagrams, design decisions, configuration references, runbooks, and operational procedures — so that the system remains supportable, auditable, and easy to onboard new engineers onto over time. Mentor and coach junior and mid-level engineers through code review, design review, pair programming, and structured knowledge sharing, helping the broader team grow in technical maturity and confidence over time. Continuously evaluate emerging IIoT technologies and platforms, assessing maturity, fit for purpose, and total cost of ownership, and make grounded recommendations on which to adopt, pilot, or defer based on real engineering needs. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical discipline. Five or more years of IIoT or industrial software engineer

jobs.title: Dirección Comercial - CAIA Ingeniería
jobs.description: Proceso de selección desarrollado por matteria Ubicación y Modalidad de Trabajo Colombia - Trabajo Remoto con viajes. Quiénes somos CAIA Ingeniería es una firma de consultoría especializada en soluciones ambientales y energéticas que acompaña a organizaciones en su transición hacia modelos de desarrollo más sostenibles. Nuestro propósito es contribuir a la transformación de las organizaciones mediante soluciones innovadoras, personalizadas y de alto impacto que fortalezcan su competitividad, incrementen su rentabilidad y generen progreso para los territorios donde operan. Como parte de nuestra Visión 2030, buscamos consolidar un crecimiento sostenido, alcanzando que el 30% de nuestros ingresos provengan de mercados de Latinoamérica y el Caribe, manteniendo una organización rentable, innovadora y centrada en las personas. Nuestro impacto Más de COP $270.000 millones gestionados en beneficios tributarios por exclusión de IVA y Renta para clientes asociados a inversiones ambientales y energéticas. Experiencia asesorando a empresas, gremios, entidades financieras y organismos públicos en mercados de carbono y financiamiento climático. Participación activa en el desarrollo de instrumentos normativos y políticas relacionadas con el cambio climático y la sostenibilidad en Colombia. Propósito del cargo Liderar la construcción y consolidación de la capacidad comercial de CAIA Ingeniería, diseñando una estrategia de crecimiento que permita diversificar la base de clientes, fortalecer el desarrollo de negocio de las diferentes unidades y acelerar la expansión hacia mercados internacionales, trabajando de manera colaborativa con los Directores Técnicos para convertir el conocimiento especializado de la organización en nuevas oportunidades de negocio. Principales Responsabilidades Estrategia comercial y crecimiento Diseñar e implementar la estrategia comercial de CAIA Ingeniería, alineada con la Visión 2030 y los objetivos de crecimiento de la organización. Construir una capacidad comercial escalable que permita ampliar la base de clientes y reducir la dependencia del negocio recurrente. Definir, junto con la Gerencia General y los Directores de Unidad, las prioridades comerciales y los mercados objetivo para cada línea de negocio. Desarrollo de nuevos negocios I dentificar, prospectar y desarrollar nuevas oportunidades comerciales en Colombia y Latinoamérica. Construir relaciones estratégicas con clientes, aliados y actores clave del ecosistema para generar oportunidades de negocio de largo plazo. Liderar la apertura de nuevos mercados e impulsar la estrategia de internacionalización de la organización, con foco inicial en Ecuador y Perú. Venta consultiva y desarrollo de propuestas Trabajar de manera articulada con los Directores de Unidad para comprender las necesidades de los clientes y diseñar propuestas de valor diferenciadas. Liderar procesos de venta consultiva, acompañando la negociación y el cierre de oportunidades junto con los equipos técnicos. Coordinar la estructuración de propuestas técnico-comerciales y modelos de pricing, asegurando que respondan a las necesidades del cliente y a los objetivos de rentabilidad de la organización. Marketing e inteligencia comercial Liderar funcionalmente el área de Marketing, integrando las estrategias de posicionamiento, generación de demanda y desarrollo comercial. Potenciar el uso de Salesforce y demás herramientas de gestión comercial para fortalecer el seguimiento de oportunidades, la toma de decisiones y la eficiencia del proceso comercial. Desarrollo de capacidades comerciales Fortalecer las competencias comerciales de los Directores de Unidad, promoviendo una cultura de desarrollo de negocios basada en la colaboración y el trabajo transversal. Diseñar e implementar metodologías, procesos e indicadores que permitan consolidar una gestión comercial sostenible y orientada a resultados. Representación y posicionamiento institucional Representar a CAIA Ingeniería ante clientes, aliados est
